page: Amazon Web Services m4.xlarge is a general-purpose, fourth-generation virtual machine instance. It is powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.4 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 4 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) mapped to 2 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, providing a dedicated CPU allocation. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU includes a 45 MB L3 cache and supports hardware flags such as AVX, AES, and FMA, though it lacks AVX-512 capabilities.

This instance operates on the Xen hypervisor and does not include any local NVMe or SSD storage, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. Network connectivity is configured with a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with the instance.

Performance benchmarks position the m4.xlarge in the lower tiers of modern cloud compute. In stress-ng and Geekbench testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 25% category. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks similarly rank in the bottom 25%. Redis SET operations and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression show poor performance, landing in the bottom 10%. However, LLM inference text generation using a small 135M model achieves an average tier rating at 71.79 tokens per second, contrasting with its weaker prompt processing speed of 184.47 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m4.xlarge represents an older hardware generation with lower resource density and lower raw performance per resource compared to newer instance families. It is best utilized for legacy general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, or light background processing tasks that do not require high-speed local storage, advanced vector instructions, or high network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services m4.xlarge is a fourth-generation general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.4 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 16.0 GB of system memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Operating on the Xen hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or bundled public IPv4 addresses. Benchmark evaluations place its CPU, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic performance largely in the bottom 25%, with redis operations and multi-threaded decompression falling into the bottom 10%. Given its older hardware profile and lower relative performance, the m4.xlarge is best suited for legacy general-purpose applications, basic web serving, and light background processing tasks that do not demand high-speed local storage or modern instruction sets.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m4.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor. It offers dedicated compute resources with a baseline network speed of 1 Gbps.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m4.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, running on the Xen hypervisor.
tagline: A dedicated general-purpose instance with balanced memory and compute resources for legacy workloads and basic web serving.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
Dedicated CPU allocation running on the Xen hypervisor
Baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps with no local storage
Benchmark performance generally ranks in the bottom 25% tier
Suited for legacy general-purpose applications and light web serving
